subject: Chemistry
title: Particle Accelerators Make Elements
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Particle Accelerators Make Elements.

hook.text: To create a new element, scientists smash atoms together at millions of miles per hour and hope they stick.

buildup.text: A particle accelerator fires ions at a target foil for weeks or months. Most collisions fail completely.

discovery.text: Occasionally, two nuclei fuse into one heavier nucleus. Detectors catch the new atom before it decays.

twist.text: Some experiments run for years to produce a single atom. Element 117 required 6 months of continuous bombardment.

climax.text: Labs in Russia, Germany, Japan, and the US compete to reach new elements. It's the modern gold rush.

punchline.text: Millions of collisions for one atom. Patience makes elements.

quiz.question: How are superheavy elements typically created?
options: By smashing atomic nuclei together in particle accelerators
By cooling gases to absolute zero
